reviews.review_description: This is certainly one of my favorite games on VR right now. I’m deeply scared of the ocean, so I knew I had to try this- and it did not disappoint. I threw my headset off more times than I can count, and I loved every second of it. The great white in the blue shark environment jump scared the crap out of me, and I accidentally teleported out of the cage in the great white map and just about died right there. And swimming up to the humpback whales was one of the most bone chilling moments I’ve ever had in VR. My only suggestions would be 1) The ability to peek above the water, even if it was just to see the surface and maybe some rocks 2) Proper gloves instead of floating ghost hands, that would feel like I’m an actual scuba diver, and 3) A proper crocodile map so we can swim with the crocs, and maybe make them aggressive too.otherwise, perfect game. 10/10.

Indeed a stellar app. Profound. In MR, multiple aquarium windows can be opened in the middle of your room and you can choose the type of aquarium at any time.
In the Jurassic tank, I recognized the elasmosaurus and the mosasaur. Leiupleuridons in Love.

It is a long road that has no turning. 

On a YouTube video, it showed a sea snake from the Indian ocean, piranhas from the Amazon and morsy eels from the Pacific Ocean all in the same area. That's like Mel Gibson's Apocalypto where Meso American tribes from different regions and time periods were all hanging out in the city square. 

Overall an ingenious app. Anyone who gets this will love it.

This is indeed a gift from Neptune aka Poseidon.

The prehistoric seas are aka shell's aquarium.

Only wish it was a tad more photorealistic. Can't complain as the graphics are better than Sony PS5 let alone the Sony PSP.

 I decided to place a few windows to the smallest size setting, there is a minimum size of about 1 foot square give or take an inch or two. 
Anyways, I lined up a few of these small square windows each evenly spaced about three feet apart. This configuration is dreamlike reminding me of at least one dream where I visited an old school public aquarium where the windows were small and set a few feet apart on the walls. There is a CBC archives video about the Vancouver aquarium in the late 60s and there was that type of architectural set up. 
Ocean Rift has a few choices for aquarium. Shallows, Swamp, Deep Sea, Prehistoric, Bélugas, Crocodiles, Sharks. 
The belugas remind me of the Vancouver aquarium. 
Some window settings go better with some animals. For bélugas and prehistoric, you need a huge bay window which is an available option as a person can draw their own windows any size. It turns an otherwise welfare rooming house single room apartment into a mind-blowing aquarium spectacular wonderland to the level of Undersea Gardens in Victoria BC back when that was going. 
The best set up is the best of both worlds. On one wall, draw up a large bay window. Then on the wall opposite, draw a few small windows, 50s and 60s style. 
The level is called swamp but it doesn't look like a swamp at all. It should be called Bc coastal waters which is quite a treat because its the waters off where I live! The waters are a brackish shade of green, The species are long green seaweed, dungeon ess crabs, moray eels, perch, white claims. 
That's not a swamp at all. A swamp is alligators and catfish and crayfish. I don't see any of these animals at all. 
Swamp is the best level for retro style small windows. So is shallows and deep sea. 
Large bay Windows is best for Prehistoric, bélugas, crocodiles, sharks. 
Deep sea has phosflorescent jellyfish and other creatures. They glow from the inside! Like angels. The water is dark and you do see the floor of the sea. 
Bélugas and sharks are the only levels that you don't see the sea floor. With all other levels, the sea floor is there. 
I only wish that Ocean Rift VR was more photorealistic in terms of stationary background like Deagle Jack VR which is a free app that's worth money. It is a Michelin star app. 

This app is a gift from God.

*Update Went back in and MR was unlocked and worked. 

WTH ????  I paid for this app BECAUSE it had mixed reality which is now removed or locked. So angry. Who updates an app by destroying it????? I want my money back!

I bought this game strictly for the Mixed Reality mode. I loaded the game and started playing. Then I find out that the Mixed Reality mode is locked for some reason? WTF? My daughter was looking forward to making her bedroom into an aquarium. I thought maybe  if I went thorough all the modules it would unlock MR Mode. NOPE!  This took just over two hours so guess what NO refund. I have seen sooooo many videos on the internet EVERY ONE of them shows this game with Mixed Reality. I don’t know who to be mad at…..the devs for locking MR or the internet for all the videos of people enjoying the MR mode.

reviews.review_title: Thanks a ton to the creator/s
reviews.review_description: Recently bought a Meta Quest 3s for my special needs child. I was a bit skeptical about the purchase. But this one App and just 2 days of her playing with it was worth all the giggles and joy she got! She feels it is real and was so anxious the first night that we had tough time putting her to bed .. next day woke up and said her Orcas are hungry and she needs to feed them breakfast! :) She even got afraid when I switched to the Great White shark! She is struggling with controllers  and might be a difficult task for us to teach her that, so I have to set it up / change it for her. Even if I / She doesn't use any other App, the past 2 days of her being so excited has already paid off the cost of the device and the App.
Awesome! So cool and fun. Love it! it's like you're in an aquarium in your own room. A little scary when a shark came in my space but no biggie. A lot of options too.

reviews.review_title: Fun to watch people play the first time.
reviews.review_description: I bought this app because of the one review a father gave. I love  the ocean,and the beauty underwater but it’s just as much fun to watch friends try it the first time. I watched my senior neighbour go from unsure to total relaxation within minutes. You could physically see the stress leave her body.  I think she would have been happy to sit there for hours watching the different types of ocean life.  Totally at peace and relaxed , something I had never seen before with her.  My other older friend jumped around like a school girl squealing in delight at the manatees.  Worth the price.

I would love to see more updates in the aquarium feature such as adding the dolphins, orcas and maybe some new animals to that feature. I would also love to see a native multiplayer option for the game in both modes. Otherwise this game is fantastic. The aquarium feature is a work of genius

I recommend this to everyone.  Lots of fun!!

(Note to developers:   Can't get whales to appear....?????)

I am not sure if it was an update or not but I think it was because I just saw crocodiles in the mixed reality mode and I really liked it that you updated the game because I never expected that, so I hope you keep updating the game small updates or big doesn’t matter. Ideas for update: you could add a swamp map, but not for the mixed reality mode but for the normal swimming mode and then you can make like green shallow water with tree trunks laying on the water and then with crocodiles and maybe anacondas, river dolphins and other fishes :). I really like your game btw.

Really a beautiful app, until some update or another made the Mixed Reality portion unusable. As finding or contacting anyone involved with this app for support is essentially a unicorn hunt, I sadly cannot recommend this app as it has lost its best feature.

I have to admit that this app is nearly perfect. The beautiful sea creatures, as well as the ability to move around smoothly, the sounds of the ocean, the unexpected Jurassic era scenes, the sounds of certain creatures, and even the mixed reality aquarium feature are all awesome.  

The only things I would work on are perhaps slowing down the sea lions a little bit because they go a little too quickly, as well as improving the graphics of some of the corals and the plants.

Also, fix that weird glitch if you’re in aquarium mode and you put on the great white shark, and then it magically appears in your space sometimes.

Last, but certainly not least, better collision detection between some of these sea creatures needs to be implemented.  Between the player, each other, and the rocks.  Sometimes they’ll just like float right through each other or through inanimate objects and that just kind of messes with the realistic essence of the whole experience. 

Oh, and one other thing…remove elements of historic human presence from the bottom of the Jurassic era sea floors.  Humans simply weren’t around then.

Regardless of these snafus, it’s a really wonderful experience, and I couldn’t foresee me or anyone owning a Meta without this app.

reviews.review_title: Perfect for showing off mixed reality...
reviews.review_description:
My review scale*
5- Amazing game, while still might not be perfect any flaws are forgivable, is well worth your time
4- Good game, might have a few minor issues or missed opportunities keeping it from being better, but is very playable and still an enjoyable experience
3- Mid tier game, not great but not awful, these games can go either way depending on who you are or what you want or expect out of the game
2- Not a very good game, but not completely broken or unplayable, you might really need to want to play this game to get any enjoyment out of it
1- Awful game, broken or unplayable, I don't see these games being for anyone unless significant updates fix the game

*This is an overly simplified review scale since meta does not allow for half stars so in some cases I may round a game up or down depending on a case by case scenario, 
my reviews are always honest and in no way swayed by anybody else and regardless if I received a game for free or not, these ratings and reviews are however part of a 
campaign for us as consumers to be better about reviewing games and helping bring the games we love the attention they deserve.

This game is an absolute masterpiece I love being able to go in the MR (Mixed Reality) and look through my windows and see an orca swimming or a group of fish. It also lets you just take a moment to relax and there is things to learn about ocean creatures on here too. I recommend!

Good fun and the MR mode was really entertaining 
Would recommend
Pls add multiplayer mode I really want to play with friends swimming around in the sea life plsssss :)

Nice graphics
But
- Information to animals only in Englisch - Setting Language to German only changes the menue and does not have any informational content 
- 2 dinausaurs to click on - does not show animates dinausaurs only a skeleton 
- 3 animals to interact with - interaction means throwing fish or rings in the water
The digital animation are not good and not close to real experience in VR. I would love my money back and uninstall
I’m just saying, I felt like I was in a small aquarium and not the ocean. But it’s a “game” so whatever.
Uninstalled the game it was boring and it didn’t let me get a refund

The mixed reality feature actually feels like you have an aquarium in your area.  This is a must have for anyone new to the quest 3 or anyone who enjoys a mixed reality experience.  This company could really cash in doing different apps like dinosaur themed one or a safari themed one.  Even a space themed one would be awesome because this app is not a very expensive app and it is a great experience.  One thing I ask of the developers is if they can update this by adding more mixed reality content, this would be even better.  Dolphins, whales, squid, and many more should be added in mixed reality to keep app fresh.  Very relaxing to have your own aquarium in your play space.  Lets get an update!!!
It should be visually better i.e. less cartoon like. I give four stars because of effort.
Good for kids

I wish they would create more apps with different themes to ambiance up your room.  Space, desert night stars, etc.  
I like to turn my room inti an aquarium then open up a screen in the middle of my underwater room and play Subnautica or any game.  For me it’s zen.
This is a good game i like it and its cool to explore
I love the cast but, you should add it so you can offer a orca a fish, I think it would be cool

I have been playing and sharing Ocean Rift for several years and it is still one of my favorites! If the developers ever do another upgrade, I would love to see 
them add Rays to the game, especially Mantas.
Such a good concept and well delivered. The extras like the Meg make this app game so cool.
Sooo realistic and fun.  The price is great for what you get.  Kids will love this.

A nice and relaxing app (or thrilling - depending on your chosen scene :)) but anachronisms spoil the atmosphere and ruin my enjoyment entirely. Some ancient temple ruins and Grimpoteuthis in the same scene with Plesiosaurs?...
Such a great app, after a hard day at work spend a bit of time 🕰️ in the aquarium and just chill, thank you 🙏
The MR part needs to have room setup, its not very fun to stand in one spot and not be able to look through all the windows you make, that's only reason there isnt a higher rating
The MR is great you can turn your room into a giant aquarium.

reviews.review_title: Fun game, please make a Land Rift game
reviews.review_description: Make another game called Land Rift which is the same as Ocean Rift but for Land. You could have a jungle where you can see jaguars, crocodiles, snakes, you could have a desert where you see scorpions, camels, you could have a spruce forest where you see foxes, rabbits, squirrels, birds, you could even have a city where you see humans, pigeons, rats, and best of all you could have an african Sahara where you see elephants, rhinos, leopards, bison, lions, cheetahs, hippos, ostriches. Please make this game🙏 I will definitely buy it
but Cthulhu is in deep sea🅱️🅾️🅱️

reviews.review_title: MR more fun than the original
reviews.review_description:
iirc I reviewed the Quest 2 version of this app several years ago.
The Quest 3 version with its mixed reality capabilities is enough of a different beast that it deserves its own review imo.
This version is fantastic. The mixed reality abilities allows you to define viewing windows in your space where your walls are located. You can see the ocean animals and environments beyond these windows, giving the impression that your room has been turned into a personal aquarium.
The  impression is sealed when something like a great white shark swims past one window and out of sight only to appear swimming past the window on another wall.
User tip 1: Make sure you set your boundary well beyond the limits of the windows. This will allow you to walk right up to the window and stare out.
User tip 2: Set up one window to overlay a doorway if you can. Then choose the option of a curved window. This will allow to not just walk right up to the window, but lean out and see what sorts of creatures are swimming just out of sight.
It's too bad that the headsets are too complicated and too large for young kids. I think they would be hugely entertained by this experience. 
Suggestion for the devs: The idea of windows in walls is a concept that could be used in so many ways. For example, it would be nice to allow users to display ambient scenes other than the ocean beyond the windows. There is a hugely popular genre of youtube videos dubbed "relaxation" that are views of nature, skylines, traffic in rain, etc. as seen through a window.
For example, how about a view from a penthouse suite of a skyline at night, with traffic visible in the streets far below and light rain running across the window?
Or, a pastoral view of Montana-like setting with the occasional buffalo, mountains in the background, birds flying past, etc.
So many possibilities exist.
Throw in the ability of the user to add their own relaxing music and I think it would be a huge hit.
Let us briefly live and relax like the ultra-wealthy do for a fraction of the cost…
Looks nice but quite boring. I shouldn’t have paid for this
HOW DID HE KNOW?!

reviews.review_title: One of the Best VR experiences
reviews.review_description: I've had Quest 3 for around 2 months now and tried many various games. Just purchased Ocean Rift today and tried it out.... Spent way too long in there. It os absolutely fantastic and so many various experiences of different species and underwater scenery. I absolutely love the aquarium setup where your walls turn into hige glass aquarium windows. Feels super real and one can sit for very long time just mesmerized. Great for kids and adults alike.
A far cry from what I was expecting, even though l knew from the start that the graphics wouldn't be the best. A massive let down for 10 euros. Uninstalled it within the first 10 mins.

I absolutely love this game , the dolphins are my fave ! However , I really am dying to experience the Aquarium but there is a lock on it. Please somebody let me know how to unlock this portion of the game ...please and thank you !

Beautiful and relaxing app that demonstrates the mixed reality and full VR scenarios as so realistically I felt excitement and surprise as real as an actual aquarium or dive. I highly recommend this for a real immersive underwater experience.

SAme graphics as the OCULUS Go version PLUS MR only works well in an empty square room.

And seriously, you can't turn with the right thumbstick.  EVEN worst, you can't turn while swimming. It only goes foward.  You can't play explore sitting down.  You have to be standing or play on a rotating chat.  

Basically, you recompiled the 2017 version. Pretty disapointing.
Why am I not able to turn 360? I could but, not now. Why? How can I fix it? Thanks.
I wish there was more to explore more underwater monsters and prehistoric animals and a virtual aquarium that you can move around in

reviews.review_title: Superb! The Mixed Reality is great!
reviews.review_description: Really love the MR part of this... on Quest 3 it's truly amazing.. need more experiences like this. Space, etc! Would love more to be done with this for the Ocean too. Maybe slightly scarier? Things hitting the glass etc. Noiser... anyways, great work!

As a VR app dating back to the HTC Vive era, this experience is one of the few offering a Mixed Reality option, which earns it a couple of points. For absolute newcomers to MR/VR, it might be interesting initially, but anyone with experience will likely find it underwhelming. The visuals are basic and outdated, with little detail or polish, and there’s no ambient sound or music to add atmosphere, making it feel incomplete and lacking in immersion.

For the current price, this app doesn’t provide enough value to justify the investment—it would be more suitable as a free or low-cost experience. While it may hold novelty appeal for first-timers, it’s ultimately limited, and anyone looking for a deeper or more refined MR experience may want to look elsewhere.

If you own a Headeat with coloured passthrough you have to try this to turn you home into an Aquarium.
It's honestly breathtaking.
I can't say much about the game but the AR experience is awsome!

The MR mode on Quest 3 is impressive, but frustrating. 

Peering through windows on your walls out into the ocean depths is surreal. The app even tints the lighting in your room based on the environment outside. It’s all very immersive until you walk up to a window for a better view and the guardian pops up, instantly breaking the spell.

Issues like this keep Quest 3’s MR from reaching its potential. (The other major problem is that most MR experiences don’t utilize the Quest 3’s laser scanner mesh model.)

The MR mode is also glitchy.  Occasionally it’ll drop a sea creature in your room and it’ll get stuck there. If they can get fix this glitch and get rid of the guardian mode in MR then it’ll be a 5 star experience.

I've only played this for 15 minutes and it looks like they had zero interest in researching what the actual animals look like. By now their profits should have covered a few updates. To save me the time giving examples just look at what they call a blue shark and actually Google what a blue shark looks like. Their version looks like an an early AI generated 'generic shark' which looks like no actual shark.

Orcas with white on the top of the flukes?
A Blue whale which looks at generic as the sharks etc.

Of you have any knowledge of any of these animals, maybe avoid. 


OK for a quick look but not worth half of what I paid.
I found in the deep sea The great old one, Cthulhu. I found out who he is and that music playing and it’s like you’re not in the deep sea it’s like you are in R’lyeh. It’s so cool and creepy

Star rating would be zero if it allowed you to choose that. The official promo video gives the impression there are going to be lots of fish and creatures swimming around you....but there's hardly anything to see!!! And to find the very few things there are you have to look around for a LONG TIME. Appalling graphics, misleading promo video, total waste of money...even if it were free I would still delete it as it's literally a waste of space.
I was hoping to see a lot of marine life like going to the aquarium but in this app you can only see a few fish at a time. Not altogether. I hope the developer can improve on this.

This is a great experience for anyone who even the slightest bit enthusiastic about the ocean or underwater environments and wildlife. The ability to “swim” or zoom around the water is great, and the ability to grab and hold certain fish is a fun and great way to inspect the details.

 I wish more marine life could be interacted with, such as crabs and starfish, but maybe that will come in the future. The amount of fish they’ve included is great, and the interactions you can have with specific marine life is a very cute touch! I will say, feeding the great white the limbs of what I presume to be cattle is a very bizarre choice. It should be said that all the feeding interactions are for entertainment, and should never be replicated as it disturbs the wildlife.

While I love the mixed reality update, I’m very disappointed with their remodel of the great white from a realistic design to a VERY cartoony design. Hopefully they revert it back. I also wish the manatees were in the swamp biome, but maybe I just didn’t get lucky for one to swim by the windows I created. Another issue with the update is, at least for me, the wrist scanner stopped working in the regular VR mode. I’m not sure why? 

It is my hope that they continue to improve and expand upon this mostly wonderful experience, even if it means bumping up the price for possible major changes, paid expansions, or a “sequel” altogether.

I read the reviews before purchasing this. I was looking forward to being eaten as it sounds exciting. But I did all of the exhibits and didn't get eaten then again I didn't find it exciting either. As a person being allergic to water so have to limit my time in it in real life. Thought this would be fun. But it definitely wasn't.  The picture quality is poor as well. Suppose it would be ok for a young child. Also took me forever to figure out how to exit it.

reviews.review_title: I thought it was a glitch, but...
reviews.review_description:
It was really just a very stupid idea on the developer's part. Every time I get near the Great White or one of the prehistoric creatures (I forget which) I keep getting eaten and sent back to the starting point for that area. There should be an option to turn that off. I've never been able to hear the info on them, because I never last that long. I also agree with the other reviewers who say 1. It is too hard to locate the info points 2. There aren't enough interactive creatures 3. Being stuck under the surface is a little irritating, especially since all of the mammals have to surface to breathe 4. The graphics could be a lot better and 5. There's too much empty water. Would also like to see a Coral Reef area.

But most of all, stop eating me! Although, that would still only raise my review to maybe 3 stars.
Good game, yummy water :)
This app is super cool. It’s an amazing feeling to swim with a whale!!! So much to explore in the ocean!
It's in French & having a difficult time changing the language.

Some of the fish look pretty good, but for the most part we're talking about graphics only slightly better than Goldeneye N64 through an RCA cable. I get that it's cheap and (somewhat) educational, and the shark cage is neat, but a ton more could've been done towards coral reefs, or just making the environment look better In general. Also there are Issues with objects; you AND fish going right through what should be solid rock or shipwrecks, arbitrary barriers with no physical object in front of you. Honestly has a strong unfinished vibe.

I haven't even got to try ocean drift yet. Downloaded it and my headset said install error. It wouldn't let me retry the install. Went back to the app to try and uninstall and reinstall but it wouldn't let me do that either. So now im stuck with a game I can't play with $10 down the drain. Customer support won't answer me to either help fix it or reimburse me.

I give it 2star rating because In all reality this was made for children and they started adding politics into the voiceover. It’s the same as teachers indoctrinating your kids at school. I would not recommend it for small children because they give their opinion and not facts. Leave the politics out of it
Not for me

I can’t recommend to people afraid of sharks and the open ocean. The cute animals and the whales are amazing but the prehistoric and deep sea are HORRIFYING lol I had to take the headset off. So obviously, they did it right! I just wish there was a way to turn off being attacked. Maybe then I would be able to venture out there. But I cannot handle a meg coming at me and eating me lol
I purchased this from Oculus in 2016. Got a new quest and purchased it again because I forgot. Why do I have to pay twice? It's an ok experience but making people rebuy is stupid.

For an educational app, this game is full of bad inaccuracies and things that have no business being in the ocean. All the rocks have the same texture applied which is taken from photos of land rocks. They are all covered in lichens, and in some places you can see (land plant) seedlings. There’s even a school of red-bellied piranhas which are exclusively fresh water fish, yet there they are, hanging out with sea snakes and cuttlefish! There are anatomical errors- the cuttlefish are rendered with two siphons but should only have one (sorry guys, don’t use tattoo art for your research next time!) The information about an animal will talk about its range in the Pacific while showing a map of the Atlantic. The seaweed is generic and a something which might be sort of a sponge is placed on the sea floor in every environment from the tropics to the deep ocean. The sea turtle is hardly bigger than a pond slider - so sad and disappointing!

I’ll also repeat what others have said- the animals and information bubbles are hard to find, the environments are sparse, and the animals don’t react to you much if at all. The only thing you can do with fish is grab (some species) in your hand and they freeze up - it feels more like you’re holding a plastic toy than a real fish. I’ve tried playing with orcas and seals which are supposed to interact, but nothing seems to happen much. It would be nice if you could hold your hand out to a fish and they would examine you or dart away and react to how you change your own behavior. It would be nice if the animals would interact with their own environment, if you could find the morays hiding in the sea floor instead moseying around in the open water. It be nice to have a richer experience with more to discover, and could learn more about ALL the species they included, not just the few large ones. 

I’d ask for a refund on this, but apparently I’ve had the app for more than two weeks.

This is relaxing and at moments can actually allows to you navigate in a convincing way that really feels like diving. 
I agree with other users that there should be some sort of dive book that allows tracking of what creatures you've sighted. That's a big thing for diving- tracking what you saw.
I'd also like to be able to swim in and through things (into ship wrecks, pipes, etc.) There was thought and detail into rendering the bottom, but you cannot interact with it. 

I enjoy the subtle way that the animals interact and follow you curiously.

Another thing that would improve my experience would be to have the animals respond to touch instead of just bouncing off of you or going straight through it. I want to pick up a sea cucumber or starfish and examine it.

Well animated!

Terrible graphics, almost no immersion, radar doesn't work, can easily go out of bounds, not as advertised, fog is annoying and you can't turn it off  and you have to go on a treasure hunt to find most animals. And even when you find the animal you're looking for it's underwhelming. Do Not Buy
UPGRADE THIS TO SUPPORT THE QUEST 2 FOR THOSE WHO HAVE ALREADY PURCHASED IT OR ELSE ITS THEFT I CAN NO LONGER PLAY IT BECAUSE SAMSUNG DISCONTINUED THE GEARVR I HOPE YOU FAIL AS A BUSINESS
Hate it

It seems like there are a lot of things to do, multiple encounters, but, in essence, it's looks like cardboard fish models put in huge space with lack of details. And there is almost no interactivity, fish doesn't react to you. Sure, you can grab them, but it's like to grab cardboard figures. Boooooring.
This gave me constant nightmares! And before I was okay with swimming in the ocean in real life! But now not anymore!

This game is impressive, I know the kids will love it.
Each animal offers a different experience
A nice introduction to VR for a first-timer like me.  Needs more content in each section and more sections really given the price.
My kids love playing it and must say myself I enjoy it as well, really real feeling. Only suggestion I give is to make interaction more detailed.

Awesome views you learn while you Exploring 
The bottom of the Ocean please make safari or zoo, beach app make games so older folks can enjoy

It feels like you're swimming with turtles, etc! You can choose which sea creature you want to swim with. You can reach out and grab a vibrant fish and look at it closely then set it free again. I wasn't able to see the whales and a couple of others that I clicked on so I'm not sure if I'm missing something or if they aren't actually there. It's very relaxing. Kids would think this is really cool!
I like it it is very good
Ideal for the younger lot but enjoyed it myself. Particularly the sea lions and dolphins which you could interact with. As an improvement it might be good to have even more of the info panels.
